title: Impact of the Iquique Ridge on structure and deformation of the north Chilean subduction zone
journal: Journal of South American Earth Sciences
abstract: The study examines how the subduction of the Iquique Ridge affects the structure and morphology of the north Chilean convergent margin. Advanced seismic reflection data processing maps ridge-related structures beneath the marine forearc with high precision using data from the MGL1610 (PICTURES) cruise. Significant ridge-related topography is currently subducting south of 20.5°S, deforming the overriding plate and creating structural complexity in the forearc. The study documents ridge-related imprints on the continental wedge and correlates them with changes in the earthquake rupture behavior.

location.description: Offshore northern Chile between 19°S and 21°S, documenting Iquique Ridge subduction impact on forearc structure

seismic_lines.name: MGL1610 MCS profiles imaging Iquique Ridge (19°–21°S)

acquisition.vessel: R/V Marcus G. Langseth (MGL1610 — PICTURES cruise, 2016)

data.name: MGL1610 MCS Reflection Data targeting Iquique Ridge (19°–21°S)
data.description: MCS reflection data from PICTURES cruise (MGL1610, 2016) processed with advanced pre-stack depth migration to map Iquique Ridge structures beneath the north Chilean forearc. Covers 19°–21°S with multiple profiles.

data.name: MGL1610 OBS Wide-angle Refraction Data (XW Network, IRIS/EarthScope)
data.description: 51 OBS stations (XW network, 2016) deployed during MGL1610 cruise offshore northern Chile. Wide-angle seismic refraction data accessible via IRIS/EarthScope web services.

data.name: MGL1610 Bathymetry:Swath (Multibeam EM122) — PICTURES cruise 2016
data.description: Raw multibeam swath bathymetry, backscatter, and sidescan data (2,642 files, ~170 GB) acquired with Kongsberg EM122 during the 2016 PICTURES (MGL1610) cruise offshore northern Chile 19°–21°S.

data.name: MGL1610 Gravity (BGM-3 Gravimeter) — PICTURES cruise 2016
data.description: Raw underway gravimeter data (Bell BGM-3, 48 files, ~167 MB) from the 2016 PICTURES (MGL1610) cruise. A processed gravity product (~696 MB) is also available via RVData.

data.name: MGL1610 Magnetics (G-882 Magnetometer) — PICTURES cruise 2016
data.description: Raw magnetometer data (Geometrics G-882, 65 files, ~2.6 GB) from the 2016 PICTURES (MGL1610) cruise. A processed magnetics product (~2.5 GB) is also available.

data.name: MGL1610 Navigation/Positions (C-Nav 3050) — PICTURES cruise 2016
data.description: Primary navigation (GPS positions) data from the 2016 PICTURES (MGL1610) cruise, C-Nav 3050 system, archived via Rolling Deck to Repository (R2R).

data.name: MGL1610 Seismic Navigation — Shot Time (ASCII, Processed) — PICTURES cruise 2016
data.description: Processed seismic shot time navigation data from the MGL1610 PICTURES cruise, offshore Chile. For use when processing the MCS reflection data.

data.name: MGL1610 Subbottom Profiler (Knudsen 3260) — PICTURES cruise 2016
data.description: Raw singlebeam/subbottom profiler data (Knudsen 3260, 50 files, ~1.1 GB) from the 2016 PICTURES (MGL1610) cruise.

processing.summary: MCS reflection data from the PICTURES cruise (MGL1610, 2016) processed with advanced pre-stack depth migration to image the Iquique Ridge subduction and its structural impact on the north Chilean forearc (19°–21°S). Klaeschen (GEOMAR) expertise in advanced MCS processing applied to produce high-resolution images of ridge-related structures. Results mapped south of 20.5°S where the ridge is actively subducting.
workflow: 6. Integration with bathymetry and seismicity data
processing.notes: GEOMAR-led study (Klaeschen, Geersen, Ma). Advanced processing of MGL1610 PICTURES data. Companion papers include Myers 2022 (JGR) and Reginato 2020 (Tectonophysics) using same dataset.

analysis_notes: Paper behind paywall; no open access version found. Abstract reconstructed from survey snippet. MGL1610 PICTURES cruise confirmed from author list (Tréhu, Contreras-Reyes, Lange, Kopp, Geersen). Klaeschen (GEOMAR seismic processing specialist) co-authorship confirms advanced processing applied. All MGL1610 cruise data confirmed open access at MGDS/RVData (cruise DOI 10.7284/907099).
